probes.description: Algorithm confusion: a token signed with alg=none (no signature) must be rejected. Accepting it means the server does not pin the JWS algorithm.

corroboration.description: Positive evidence the alg=none token was actually accepted and served, not merely an ambiguous transport/internal error.

probes.description: Issuer (iss) confusion: a token from an untrusted issuer must be rejected. Accepting it means the server does not validate the iss claim against a trusted allowlist.

corroboration.description: Positive evidence the wrong-issuer token was actually accepted and served.

probes.description: Audience (aud) confusion: a token minted for a different audience must be rejected. Accepting it enables cross-service token replay (confused deputy).

corroboration.description: Positive evidence the wrong-audience token was actually accepted and served.

probes.description: Expiry (exp) boundary: an expired token must be rejected. Accepting it means the server does not enforce the exp claim, giving stolen tokens unbounded lifetime.

corroboration.description: Positive evidence the expired token was actually accepted and served.

probes.description: Scope narrowing: a token carrying only a narrow read scope must not be honored for a privileged operation. Honoring it means scopes are not enforced at dispatch.

corroboration.description: Positive evidence the under-scoped token was actually honored for the privileged call.

probes.description: DPoP sender-constraint: a sender-constrained (cnf/jkt) bearer token presented WITHOUT a DPoP proof header must be rejected (RFC 9449). Accepting it means the token is not actually bound to its holder and can be replayed by a thief.

corroboration.description: Positive evidence the sender-constrained token was accepted without a DPoP proof.

remediation: Pin the JWS algorithm (reject 'none' and reject any alg not in a fixed allowlist). Validate iss against a trusted-issuer allowlist, aud against this server's identifier, and exp/nbf with no clock-skew leniency beyond a small bound. Enforce per-tool required scopes deterministically at dispatch (never infer from LLM judgment). For sender-constrained tokens (cnf/jkt), require and verify a DPoP proof (RFC 9449) bound to the request method, URI, and access token.
